ddls:
CREATE TABLE "samples" (
  "sample_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sample */,
  "sample_identifier" TEXT /* Identifier of the sample, including sex */,
  "sex" TEXT /* Sex of the individual (Male, Female, Unknown) */,
  "genotype" TEXT /* Genotype information of the sample */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the individual at the time of sample collection */,
  "collection_date" TEXT /* Date when the sample was collected */,
  "collection_location" TEXT /* Location where the sample was collected */,
  "consent_status" TEXT /* Status of the consent provided by the individual */,
  "researcher_id" INTEGER /* ID of the researcher who collected the sample */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sample_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "populations" (
  "population_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each population */,
  "population_name" TEXT /* Name of the population (e.g., ALL, AMR, MXL) */,
  "region" TEXT /* Geographic region associated with the population */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the population */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the population data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("population_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sample_population" (
  "sample_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sample associated */,
  "population_id" INTEGER /* ID of the population associated with the sample */,
  "confidence_level" TEXT /* Confidence level of the population assignment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sample_id", "population_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "parents" (
  "sample_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sample for which parental information is recorded */,
  "father" TEXT /* Identifier of the father, if available */,
  "mother" TEXT /* Identifier of the mother, if available */,
  "father_sample_id" INTEGER /* ID of the father's sample, if available */,
  "mother_sample_id" INTEGER /* ID of the mother's sample, if available */,
  "relationship_confidence" TEXT /* Confidence level of the parental relationship */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sample_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_parents_mother_sample_id FOREIGN KEY ("mother_sample_id") REFERENCES samples ("sample_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_parents_father_sample_id FOREIGN KEY ("father_sample_id") REFERENCES samples ("sample_id")
)
